_comment: FB tranche-A∪B evaluation. Four configs: BLF and NoBel × {Pro-3.1, Sonnet-4.6}, all at the SOTA setting (crowd=1, prior=1, tools=1). The 2×2 answers two reviewer concerns at once: (1) generalizability — does the BLF architecture work on a non-Google frontier LLM (Sonnet)? (2) belief-state isolation — is the structured belief state the value-add, or is it just the base LLM? NoBel rows ablate the structured belief state (text accumulation only), giving an AIA-/Cassi-style baseline at the same crowd+emp+tools level as BLF; the Δ within each LLM directly measures the belief-state contribution.
